statement.focus_question: Let\[A = \left\{ \sum_{n\in S}n! : S\subset \mathbb{N}\textrm{ finite}\right\}.\]If $k\geq 2$, then does $A$ contain only finitely many $k$th powers?
statement.normalized: Let\[A = \left\{ \sum_{n\in S}n! : S\subset \mathbb{N}\textrm{ finite}\right\}.\]If $k\geq 2$, then does $A$ contain only finitely many $k$th powers? Does it contain only finitely many powerful numbers?
statement.original: Let\[A = \left\{ \sum_{n\in S}n! : S\subset \mathbb{N}\textrm{ finite}\right\}.\]If $k\geq 2$, then does $A$ contain only finitely many $k$th powers? Does it contain only finitely many powerful numbers?
